items.summary: South Sydney captain Cam Murray praised his side for being able to focus on the field as the league celebrated the "world's biggest birthday party" for Jai Arrow.

items.title: 'Can't knock Zach': Dons coach defends Merrett despite horror showing
items.summary: The Brisbane Lions have taken a big win over Essendon to snatch a top-four spot after round 18, as Melbourne and the Western Bulldogs enjoy their respective victories. Follow live.

items.title: World Cup: Bellingham goes big time again, as England continues down hard road
items.summary: Jude Bellingham has been scrutinised and criticised in the months leading up to the World Cup. Now he stands as England's best player so far, carrying the Three Lions' title dreams on his shoulders.

items.title: World Cup controversy as Norway fumes over England's spidercam-assisted equaliser
items.summary: Norway felt England's equaliser shouldn't have stood after the ball appeared to get a deflection off a wire holding up the "spidercam" above the pitch, directly leading to England gaining possession and scoring.

items.title: Love story: Motherhood is helping Moana Hope re-learn how to love
items.summary: The former AFLW player was an adult when she realised her childhood had warped her understanding of love.

items.title: China ballistic missile test pushes forward case for Pacific Security Agreement
items.summary: Pacific Minister Pat Conroy says the testing of a nuclear-capable missile damaged China's reputation and has strengthened the case for a Pacific security pact.

items.title: Man charged with trespass after filming inside Dezi Freeman's shipping container hideout
items.summary: A man who entered the property where Dezi Freeman had been hiding out in north-east Victoria has been charged with trespass.

items.title: Junior Perth doctor, 26, charged over death near Fremantle's entertainment precinct
items.summary: A junior doctor from Perth's western suburbs appears in court charged with manslaughter following the death of a 63-year-old man near Fremantle's entertainment strip in the early hours yesterday.

items.title: Numbat conservation efforts pull native species from brink of extinction
items.summary: Numbats, Western Australia's animal emblem, have been pulled from the brink of extinction after research found the population was growing in the wild.

items.title: Horsham footballer 'alert' in Melbourne hospital after on-field head knock
items.summary: A young regional footballer who sustained a head knock and concussion during a match on Saturday is recovering well in a Melbourne hospital, according to his club.

items.title: What the Australia-India uranium deal could mean for Western Australia
items.summary: Australia has struck a uranium exports deal with India, which has put WA’s effective ban on uranium mining and exporting in the spotlight.

items.title: Wajarri man Kurt McIntosh picked up a book at an airport. It transformed his life
items.summary: Board meetings and navigating native title were not on Kurt McIntosh's radar until he stumbled upon a book at the airport.

items.title: Two stabbed after gatecrashers spark violent brawl in Sydney's south-west
items.summary: NSW Police are investigating after a man and a boy were found with stab wounds in Sydney's south-west, while two teens were injured in a separate incident involving a large brawl in Sydney's north-west.

items.title: How a fireman helped make dinosaur fossils cool again
items.summary: Travis Enright is four months into investigating what he calls "a 100-million-year-old cold case" involving a 10-metre-long aquatic dinosaur called Moria.

items.title: NT government faces Federal Court challenge over laws capping compensation payouts
items.summary: The NT government's controversial laws capping compensation for prisoners unlawfully assaulted, strip searched or imprisoned are being challenged in the Federal Court.

items.title: Artist Julie Aldridge's 10-year battle to reclaim mosaic design from the internet
items.summary: A South Australian artist is fighting drop-ship company Temu over its selling of her mosaic design on rugs. But how did the artwork get there in the first place?

items.title: Cost-conscious Canberra cyclists continue bracing against winter's chill
items.summary: Every year, winter sees many Canberrans hanging up their helmets, despite the city being considered Australia's "cycling capital." This year though, cyclist numbers have remained relatively steady, even as the mercury drops.

items.title: Ensay's football homecoming revives decades-old legacy
items.summary: A special tribute match at Ensay's oval in regional Victoria resurrected a football tradition and brought generations of a Gippsland high country community together.

items.title: Historic Brickendon Estate turns traditional Sunday roast into paddock-to-plate agritourism experience
items.summary: A 200-year-old Tasmanian farm is opening its gates to diners for the first time, as the family-run business seeks to expand its offerings and keep on top of costly upkeep.

items.title: Invisible string that Australia attached to PNG's rugby league team
items.summary: In a contest for Pacific influence against China, Australia's football diplomacy comes with risks and rewards.

items.title: Fake mountain, MONA-style hub and Aboriginal gallery among old RAH plans that never materialised
items.summary: Adelaide's stalled Aboriginal cultural centre has a dubious future on a site with a troubled past. The Malinauskas government faces criticism whatever it chooses to do, writes state political reporter Thomas Kelsall.

items.title: Socceroos star Jordy Bos sidelined for months after undergoing successful knee surgery
items.summary: Jordy Bos goes under the knife after his World Cup campaign came to an abrupt end due to a knee injury against Egypt.

items.title: Linda Nosková survives Karolína Muchová fightback in all-Czech final to lift Wimbledon women's singles trophy
items.summary: Linda Nosková becomes the third Czech woman in four years to claim the Wimbledon singles title as she downs her great friend in the final.

items.title: Rare glimpse into Discovery expedition helps scientist better understand the present
items.summary: Hidden for more than a century, a remarkable collection is offering a rare glimpse into the famed 1900s Discovery expedition to Antarctica.

items.title: Grace Woodroofe on Heath Ledger, healing and turning abuse into art for new album
items.summary: Heath Ledger launched her music career at just 17, but an emotionally abusive relationship with a fellow musician derailed it. A decade on, Grace Woodroofe is releasing the soundtrack to her survival.

items.title: Telstra outage fallout will put overseas holidays on the backburner for anxious leaders
items.summary: For Australian politicians, Scott Morrison's ill-fated Hawaii vacation in December 2019 remains a potent reminder of what not to do in a national crisis.

items.title: Israeli court battle threatens to turn into constitutional crisis
items.summary: A Trumpian clash between the Netanyahu government and Israel's most senior court is rapidly building into a major constitutional crisis.

items.title: As Japan's population shrinks, bears are moving close to town and creating a big problem
items.summary: When Araki Osamu first heard reports a bear had been seen in the streets of his home town, a 1-hour bullet train ride north of Tokyo, he dismissed it as "fake news".

items.title: Australia's first map of city zoning shows Melbourne streets ahead on density
items.summary: Four years on from a national pledge to unlock more housing, only one capital city allows townhouses or three-storey developments on most of its residential land.

items.title: Can Australia avoid the next round of the global energy crisis?
items.summary: As the conflict has dragged on, gas prices have soared and now threaten to up-end the economies of Europe and Asia.

items.title: Devon, polony, fritz: The food that goes by a half dozen different names
items.summary: The words we use for a handful of classic Australian foods can tell a lot about where we come from.

items.title: The Invite welcomes you to one of the best films of 2026
items.summary: Olivia Wilde's return to directing might take place almost entirely in a single room, but it unfolds all the relationship anxieties we prefer to keep hidden at home.

items.title: Speedboat capsize off Vietnam's Phu Quoc island kills 15 tourists
items.summary: A tourist boat returning from an island trip in southern Vietnam has capsized, killing 15 Indian visitors, state media has reported.

items.title: France dominates Wallabies to post historic Test win
items.summary: Following a second-half blitz, France scores its fourth straight victory over the Wallabies, triumphing 42-26 in the Nations Championship Test in Brisbane.

items.title: Police lockdown of Emu Park neighbourhood lifted
items.summary: Police declared a public emergency after shots were reportedly fired at a property in Emu Park, central Queensland.

items.title: Scarborough MP Stuart Aubrey's office vandalised in 'targeted incident'
items.summary: Scarborough MP Stuart Aubrey says his office was "deliberately damaged" by a vandal on Friday night.

items.title: Telstra will face Senate inquiry after nationwide outage
items.summary: The telco will face a grilling from politicians about this week's nationwide outage that brought businesses and transport systems into chaos.

items.title: 'Lost feeling down one arm': Dogs coach reveals extent of Crichton injury
items.summary: Canterbury captain Stephen Crichton is removed from the the Bulldogs' 40-16 loss to the Raiders on a medicab, with coach Cameron Ciraldo confirming a recurrence of nerve issues in his neck and shoulder.

items.title: Man, 74, critical after being assaulted on early morning walk in Yennora
items.summary: A 74-year-old man is in a critical condition after being assaulted in Western Sydney.

items.title: Bail refused for man accused of damaging EV charging stations across the Central Coast and Newcastle
items.summary: Dylan Hyland, 29, is facing 26 charges relating to the theft of copper cables from EV chargers in the NSW Central Coast and Newcastle regions.

items.title: Meta pulls new Instagram AI feature after widespread backlash
items.summary: The automatic feature, integrated into its Meta AI chatbot, let users edit generated images directly through sketches.

items.title: Jarome Luai to leave Wests Tigers at end of season
items.summary: Wests Tigers have confirmed star five-eighth Jarome Luai will leave the club at the end of the season.

items.title: Hardwick lashes 'peanut' football fans after fiery run-in with spectator
items.summary: Damien Hardwick clashes with Crows fans at Adelaide Oval, as Jeremy Cameron's injury timeline is revealed.

items.title: Young Indonesians fear for future after startup founder jailed
items.summary: The sentencing of tech entrepreneur Nadiem Makarim leaves many young Indonesians questioning whether investing in their country could expose them to legal risks.

items.title: Carbon monoxide poisoning suspected in fatal Mount Gambier incident
items.summary: A 55-year-old has died and three other men have been taken to hospital after a case of suspected carbon monoxide poisoning at a Mount Gambier home in South Australia's south-east.

items.title: Details emerge about 16 children who lived in Ohio house of horrors
items.summary: From moving around southern Ohio without leaving a paper trail, to being married when they were teenagers, details are emerging about the Siders family.

items.title: Hepburn Shire mayor says charges in 'private prosecution' by resident a 'threat to democracy'
items.summary: The mayor of Hepburn Shire in Victoria is the third council representative to be charged by the same resident via a rare "private prosecution".

items.title: A warming veggie and lentil soup that's ready in less than 30 minutes - ABC News
items.summary: This easy, healthy, pantry-based soup is a saviour if your fridge is looking bare.

items.title: Remembering the quadriplegic artist who drew the outback without using his hands
items.summary: Left quadriplegic in an accident, every line the late Gunggari man drew and every word he scrawled in ink was created without a pen in his hands.

items.title: WA Police arrest two men after man's body found on South Terrace, Fremantle
items.summary: Police have arrested two men in their 20s after a man was found unresponsive near Fremantle's nightlife precinct overnight.

items.title: Polling shows Labor is still sliding in Tasmania, despite facing a 12-year-old minority government
items.summary: When Labor recorded its lowest electoral result in over a century last year some may have felt the party had hit rock bottom, at least in terms of polling. That may not be entirely the case, writes Lucy MacDonald.

items.title: Derryn Hinch's memorable media moments: from radio wars with prime ministers to Dancing with the Stars
items.summary: A man of colourful contradictions, Derryn Hinch was a larger-than-life fixture on Australian radio and television over five decades who died on Friday morning at the age of 82.

items.title: Broken Hill mother exposed to lead as a child fights same battle with her children
items.summary: Baylee Stokes lives with the health impacts of being exposed to lead as a child. Now, she's considering moving away from her hometown to protect her own children.

items.title: Living in the 'forgotten' community of Beagle Bay
items.summary: Residents of the Indigenous community of Beagle Bay say they feel forgotten by their landlord: the Western Australian government.

items.title: Aboriginal artist combining centuries-old glassblowing technique with millennia-old traditional weaving practice
items.summary: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

items.title: ACT government signs deal to buy CSIRO's Ginninderra site, plans to build 3,000 new homes
items.summary: After being flagged for development over a decade ago, the ACT government has reached an agreement for the sale of land at a former CSIRO agriculture research site to construction of thousands new homes.

items.title: Fears workers will never receive entitlement from GFG's liquidated companies
items.summary: Jun Samonte has spent 14 years working for the same Whyalla business. He now fears he will never receive the $53,000 he is owed in entitlements, despite already waiting a year.

items.title: H5 bird flu poses risk to SA's Coorong critical wetlands
items.summary: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

items.title: Cruz Hewitt becomes first Australian since Alex de Minaur to reach Wimbledon boys' final
items.summary: Cruz Hewitt continues his dominant run at Wimbledon, keeping his straight sets record intact en-route to the boys' final.

items.title: Handmade quilts weave culture into Forster NAIDOC Week baby celebration
items.summary: Beautiful Indigenous-themed quilts have been made by a group of women for a NAIDOC Week baby celebration on Worimi country, linking cultures and generations.

items.title: NT Police release new footage of Bradley John Murdoch ahead of 25th anniversary of Falconio mystery
items.summary: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final weeks, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of British tourist Peter Falconio's remains.
